name: multi-clip-drag-should-20260406-0421
idea: Multi-clip drag should support creating new tracks above/below the timeline, just like single-clip drag does. Currently: single-clip cross-track drag checks dropPosition.isNewTrack and calls createTrackAndMoveClip (useClipDrag.ts:364-367). Multi-clip drag explicitly skips isNewTrack (line 383) and planMultiDragMoves returns canMove:false if any clip target index goes out of bounds (multi-drag-utils.ts:71-72). Fix: when the anchor clip's drop position is isNewTrack, create the new track first, then plan multi-drag moves using the updated row list that includes the new track. Key files: useClipDrag.ts (drag handler), multi-drag-utils.ts (planMultiDragMoves, applyMultiDragMoves), useTimelineTrackManagement.ts (createTrackAndMoveClip). The anchor clip determines the new track position (top/bottom). Secondary clips maintain their relative track offsets from the anchor. Must handle: clips spanning multiple source tracks where some would land on new tracks, track kind compatibility, and the drop indicator showing correctly during multi-drag near edges.

overrides.note: User context: Q1: Reject multi-drag to new track when any secondary clip would need an additional track that doesn't exist. Don't create multiple new tracks — just one new track for the anchor, and if other clips can't fit, reject. Q2: The drop indicator should show the same new-track affordance during multi-drag as it does for single-clip drag. Q3: Use whatever approach works best — the plan author should determine the right edit path.
